threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look confused when the Overground’s running late. Locals just shrug and grab a coffee instead.
Caught a lad on the 35 arguing with a bag of chips like it owed him money. Brixton at 3 AM really brings out the characters.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a kid selling lemonade outside the Türkenmarkt, haggling like a pro while the parents sipped coffee nearby. So Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg do you find a late-night Späti that doubles as an art gallery, with local artists showcasing their work right next to the beer shelves.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s local when they know the difference between a popping sound from Civic Center and a firework vs. just another train malfunction.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a full tuxedo get on the Muni with a bouquet, only to step out minutes later to chase after a runaway dog. Classic SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often think Gràcia is just a trendy spot for brunch, but they miss the hidden plazas where locals gather for a game of petanca.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by their excitement at discovering the 'merienda' tradition, asking for hot chocolate at 5 PM, completely unaware of the late dinner culture.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think the 'launderette' is a boutique coffee shop instead of a place to wash your clothes. Good luck explaining that one!
Last night on the 196, a guy in a parrot costume was holding court. The bus turned into a mini circus—complete with bad jokes about feathers.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a whole group of kids selling homemade lemonade outside the cemetery, chatting with people passing by like it was the most normal thing in the world.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg will you find a random Döner stand that doubles as an art gallery at night. Street art meets late-night cravings.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s local when they’ve got a favorite taco truck and can name at least three BART outages from the past month.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy serenading the Muni crowd with a ukulele at Civic Center—had everyone clapping until the train pulled up and he vanished.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often miss that the best vermut is had at the tiny bars nestled along Carrer de Perill, not on the crowded Plaça del Sol.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell by their bewilderment at local festivals; they're the ones staring at the castellers with wide eyes, wondering if it's a circus act.
